description: A sub-interface is not supported as an underlay for EVPN-VXLAN.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Sub-Interface?
Feature Caveat: Sub-Interface - A sub-interface is not supported as an underlay for EVPN-VXLAN.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When using the REST v10.04 API to retrieve the value of pfc_priorities_config and pfc_priorities_applied columns from the Interface table, the API returns NULL values irrespective of the switch configuration.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for QoS?

Feature Caveat: QoS - When using the REST v10.04 API to retrieve the value of pfc_priorities_config and pfc_priorities_applied columns from the Interface table, the API returns NULL values irrespective of the switch configuration.

description: IPV6 vxlan Tunnels on 8325 and 10000 Switch series are supported with only ROP ports as an underlay. MCLAG or LAG as an underlay is not supported.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - IPV6 vxlan Tunnels on 8325 and 10000 Switch series are supported with only ROP ports as an underlay. MCLAG or LAG as an underlay is not supported.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: BFD sessions are not supported on sub interfaces. Use a switch virtual interfaces (SVI) to configure a BFD session.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Subinterfaces?
Feature Caveat: Subinterfaces - BFD sessions are not supported on sub interfaces. Use a switch virtual interfaces (SVI) to configure a BFD session.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

description: Symptom: Static routes are deleted after an upgrade from AOS-CX 10.08.1010 to later versions. Scenario: This issue is observed when, the switch had static routes with 2 or more next-hops for the same prefix the switch had static routes with 3 next-hops for the same prefix and the static routes for the first two next-hop were deleted. Workaround: Apply the missing static route again.

description: Symptom: Firmware updates via Aruba Central fails with an event log, Event | 4403 | LOG_ERR | CDTR | 1 | User admin: primary image update failed via HTTPS from common.cloud.hpe.com. Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch is configured to use http-proxy and the switch's upstream DNS server does not have an entry for common.cloud.hpe.com and when DNS recursion is disabled. Workaround: Configure a static DNS entry for common.cloud.hpe.com using the ip dns host command.

description: Symptom: Auth request flooding is seen due to continuous MAC moves when a dual uplink AP is connected to the switch. Scenario: This issue is observed when the dual uplink AP connected to switch ports sends LLDP frames on both the ports with concurrent onboarding and device profile enabled. Workaround: Disable concurrent onboarding and disable LLDP authentication, or remove the device profile and disable LLDP authentication.
symptom: Auth request flooding is seen due to continuous MAC moves when a dual uplink AP is connected to the switch.
scenario: This issue is observed when the dual uplink AP connected to switch ports sends LLDP frames on both the ports with concurrent onboarding and device profile enabled.
workaround: Disable concurrent onboarding and disable LLDP authentication, or remove the device profile and disable LLDP authentication.

description: Symptom: Clients are unable to receive the IP address when DHCP snooping is enabled and when the source MAC address is different from the client hardware address. Scenario: Ethernet MAC address and client hardware address are diffrent for the clients. This issue is observed when DHCPv4 snooping drops the DHCP offer / DHCP acknowledgment packets when the source MAC address differs from the client MAC address field in the DHCP discover / request packets. This issue is seen even when the verify MAC option is disabled. Workaround: Configure the client hardware address as static MAC address in the MAC table.
symptom: Clients are unable to receive the IP address when DHCP snooping is enabled and when the source MAC address is different from the client hardware address.
scenario: Ethernet MAC address and client hardware address are diffrent for the clients. This issue is observed when DHCPv4 snooping drops the DHCP offer / DHCP acknowledgment packets when the source MAC address differs from the client MAC address field in the DHCP discover / request packets. This issue is seen even when the verify MAC option is disabled
workaround: Configure the client hardware address as static MAC address in the MAC table.

This issue is seen even when the verify MAC option is disabled. Workaround: Configure the client hardware address as static MAC address in the MAC table.

workaround: Configure the client hardware address as static MAC address in the MAC table.

description: Symptom: Some devices were unable to reach resources on other VTEPs. Scenario: This issue is observed in rare scenarios when a few ARP entries are in bad state. Workaround: Delete and add static ARP entries for the neighbor that did not see ARP entries.

workaround: Delete and add static ARP entries for the neighbor that did not see ARP entries.

description: Symptom: On border leaf VTEPs, users might observe that the ARP entries for the physical IP address were properly populated to the new VTEP IP address, whereas the Virtual IP address might still point to the older VTEP IP address. Scenario: This issue is observed when ARP entries on the border leaf VTEPs fail to update ARP entries for VIP of the client after a VMotion was performed. Workaround: Moving the client again from new VTEP to the old VTEP.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
